title: Individuals and families experiencing poverty often face significant challenges when it comes to meeting their health an…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Individuals and families experiencing poverty often face significant challenges when it comes to meeting their health and behavioral health needs such as having limited access to quality healthcare, struggling to afford basic necessities, and dealing with the emotional toll or stress that is frequently caused by their situation. Accessing quality healthcare often involves an individual to have insurance or be forced to take on a financial burden that cannot be sustained. As a result, many individuals are not able to prioritize their health and struggle to develop healthy habits. Likewise, individuals/families may struggle to afford basic necessities such as food, housing, and utilities. This can lead to emotional distress which may increase the risk of these individuals developing chronic diseases. It is important to understand the impact that poverty may have on one’s mental health in order to properly assist them.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Unfortunately, the cycle of poverty and poor health can often be difficult to break. For example, if an individual is unable to afford healthy food options, they may be at a higher risk for developing chronic health conditions such as diabetes or heart disease. Additionally, the stress and emotional toll of living in poverty can lead to mental health issues such as depression and anxiety.

One way to address these challenges is to increase access to affordable health and behavioral healthcare services, as well as support services such as affordable housing and job training. Additionally, addressing the root causes of poverty such as lack of education and unequal access to employment opportunities can also play a role in improving health outcomes.

Overall, it is important to recognize the connection between poverty and poor health outcomes in order to address the underlying issues and provide support to individuals and families in need. By addressing poverty and improving access to healthcare, we can work towards creating a healthier and more equitable society.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I quickly learned that financial struggle meant compromising

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
many aspects of my health. When I was in college, I did not have enough money to pay for nutritious food, so I often turned to unhealthy options that were cheap and readily available. This led to me gaining weight and experiencing fatigue and other health issues. I also struggled to afford quality healthcare and had to put off necessary medical appointments and treatments.

Additionally, the stress and emotional toll of constantly worrying about money and making ends meet took a toll on my mental health. I found myself feeling anxious and overwhelmed most of the time, which affected my ability to focus on my studies and resulted in poor academic performance.

It was only once I started making enough money to afford basic necessities and access quality healthcare that I was able to prioritize my health and work towards improving it. I started focusing on eating healthier foods, exercising regularly, and addressing my mental health needs through therapy and self-care.

My experience has shown me how interconnected financial stability and health are, and how difficult it can be to break the cycle of poverty and poor health outcomes. It is crucial to provide support and resources to individuals and families facing financial struggles in order to help them access the care and resources they need to improve their health and wellbeing.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: The speakers also discussed how hard it was to reach out for help due to embarrassment and unawareness of their options. As a pharmacist I want to ensure that I am educated on the resources that can be provided to my patients. As an interprofessional team I want to be mindful of using gentle verbal and nonverbal communication so that patients feel comfortable opening up to me about their personal lives.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As a pharmacist, it is important to be knowledgeable about the resources available to your patients who may be experiencing financial struggles and related health issues. This could include providing information on programs such as Medicaid, the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), or community health clinics that offer low-cost or free medical services.

In addition, it is important for healthcare professionals to create a welcoming and non-judgmental environment for patients to feel comfortable discussing their personal lives and experiences. This can involve using gentle and empathetic communication, actively listening to patients’ concerns, and providing support and encouragement.

Working as part of an interprofessional team, pharmacists can collaborate with other healthcare professionals to address the various needs of patients experiencing poverty and related health issues. This may involve referring patients to social workers, case managers, or financial counselors who can provide additional support and resources.

Overall, pharmacists and healthcare professionals play a vital role in addressing the challenges that many individuals and families facing poverty encounter when it comes to accessing quality healthcare and other necessary resources. By working together and providing support and resources to those in need, we can help break the cycle of poverty and improve health outcomes for all.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: The social determinants of health relating to healthcare access, neighborhoods, and social/community support all have an impact on an individual’s ability to receive care and communicate their needs. As discussed in the synchronous session, there were many gaps in the system preventing individuals from receiving community support. As a result, volunteers are a necessity for those who cannot afford transportation to assist in transporting groceries for example.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
You are absolutely right. Social determinants of health such as access to healthcare, neighborhood conditions, and social/community support can significantly impact an individual's ability to access care and communicate their needs. Lack of access to transportation, for instance, can make it difficult for individuals to get to medical appointments or pick up medication from the pharmacy.

As discussed in the synchronous session, there often are gaps in the system that prevent individuals from receiving the community support they need. This is where volunteers can play a critical role in helping to bridge these gaps and provide much-needed support to individuals experiencing poverty. For example, volunteers can provide transportation services to individuals who cannot afford it or assist with tasks such as grocery shopping or delivering meals to those who are homebound.

Moreover, volunteers can also help to build meaningful relationships with individuals who may be isolated or struggling to make connections within their community. This type of social/community support can be invaluable for individuals experiencing poverty as it can improve their mental and emotional well-being, reduce feelings of isolation, and increase their sense of belonging.

Overall, volunteers are an integral part of the healthcare system and can play a crucial role in ensuring that individuals and families facing poverty have access to the support and resources they need to improve their health and well-being.
